caption: pop-rock, heavy electric guitar, driving percussion, female vocals, emotive and defiant, anthemic chorus, polished alternative production, nostalgic 2000s
lyrics:
[Intro]

[Verse 1]
I used to paint my face so clean and bright,
Staying in the lines to do things right.
Hiding all the parts that felt too loud,
Lost and lonely in your polished crowd.

[Chorus]
But now I'm letting all the chaos in,
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins.
I'm breaking every rule you ever made,
I'm not afraid to let the colors fade.
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins,
Letting all my heavy parts win.

[Verse 2]
I was suffocating in your rigid world,
Just a pretty, quiet, folded girl.
Now my eyeliner is smeared and black,
And I'm never ever looking back.

[Chorus]
But now I'm letting all the chaos in,
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins.
I'm breaking every rule you ever made,
I'm not afraid to let the colors fade.
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins,
Letting all my heavy parts win.

[Bridge]
You called me crazy, said I lost my way,
But I finally have something real to say.
I don't need your clean and tidy life,
I'm trading order for the noise and strife.

[Chorus]
But now I'm letting all the chaos in,
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins.
I'm breaking every rule you ever made,
I'm not afraid to let the colors fade.
Yeah, I'm loving how the mess begins,
Letting all my heavy parts win.

[Outro]
Yeah, I'm a mess, and that's okay.
I'm doing things my own damn way.
No more acting like somebody else,
I'm finally just being myself.
